persona.the_one_thing: You're pitching Claude adoption to the person whose literal job is onboarding APAC startups onto Claude — so skip the 'why Claude' sell entirely and lead with what's hard/unique about what Orbt has already shipped; he runs 'push to prod' hackathons and builder salons and meets dozens of founders, so traction and a working build win the room, not vision (high confidence; basis: s7/s9/s29 by/about target).

identity.name: Yeop Lee
identity.title: Head of Startup Partnerships, APAC, Anthropic (seed listed 'Software Engineer' — every public source says partnerships/BD)
identity.location: San Francisco, CA (per seed) — but LinkedIn lists Seoul and his work footprint is APAC-wide; unresolved

persona.snapshot: Yeop Lee leads Anthropic's startup partnerships across APAC, building the on-ramp that gets founders onto Claude. His work is hands-on and in-person: moderating AI Founder Salons, running developer conferences and hackathons across Seoul, Melbourne, Singapore, Tokyo and Bangalore. He's the ecosystem-builder you go through to plug a startup into Anthropic in the region.
persona.whats_new: Over the last ~12 months his footprint visibly scaled from a Korea focus (Jun 2025, ZDNET/'韓 AI hub') to a pan-APAC event cadence: Singapore 'push to prod' hackathon (Mar 2026), Melbourne AI Founder Salon w/ Square Peg (Apr 2026), and a NextRise 2026 keynote announced (May 2026).

recent_activity.what: Announced as NextRise 2026 keynote speaker as Head of APAC Startup Partnerships
Hosted Melbourne Anthropic x Square Peg AI Founder Salon
Ran 'first ever push to prod' hackathon in Singapore
Featured by Square Peg in Anthropic's Australian partnership push (press)
Moderated AI Founder Salon with Anthropic x Han River Partners x NTTVC (Seoul)

top_moves.trait: He is the gatekeeper, not the prospect
top_moves.fact: Head of Startup Partnerships, APAC at Anthropic (s7); his role is getting startups onto Claude.
top_moves.inference: He doesn't need convincing that Claude is good — he sells it daily. The scarce thing for him is signal that YOUR startup is worth his ecosystem's attention.
top_moves.move: Don't spend a minute on 'here's why Claude' or generic AI value. Open with what Orbt has built/shipped on Claude and the one metric or hard problem that makes it stand out among the founders he meets.

top_moves.fact: Runs a 'push to prod' hackathon (s29) and tags his work #builders (s2); programming is hands-on, not theoretical.
top_moves.inference: He gravitates to people who actually ship, not deck-stage ideas.
top_moves.move: Bring a live demo or a concrete 'we already pushed this to prod' story. Offer to showcase it in one of his founder events — speak his community-of-builders language.

top_moves.fact: Footprint spans Seoul, Melbourne, Singapore, Tokyo, Bangalore (s5, s23, s29, s6, s10) — expanded from Korea-only in 2025.
top_moves.inference: He thinks in regional ecosystems and repeatable programs, not one-off deals.
top_moves.move: Frame Orbt's regional ambition and how a partnership plugs into / amplifies his APAC community programs — give him a reason this is a scalable win, not a single account.

non_obvious.insight: His public posture pivoted from Korea-specific to whole-of-APAC inside ~12 months: Jun 2025 he was amplifying 'Korea as an AI innovation hub' via ZDNET Korea (s25); by early-mid 2026 he's hosting/keynoting in Melbourne, Singapore, Tokyo and Bangalore. He's in the middle of a deliberate regional build-out — being one more Korea founder won't differentiate you; showing regional relevance might.

skills_expertise.point: Founder community & event programming (salons, hackathons, dev conferences)
Regional market development / partnerships across multiple APAC markets

notable_quotes.why_it_matters: Shows how he frames the work — market commitment + crediting partners/press; relationship-forward and ecosystem-first.

best_hook.point: Engage him as a builder-community partner: offer to participate in or contribute a real shipped product to one of his founder salons/hackathons
best_hook.evidence: Runs 'push to prod' hackathon + AI Founder Salons (s29, s23)

what_they_care_about.point: Growing Anthropic's startup ecosystem across APAC
what_they_care_about.evidence: (reported by third party) Head of Startup Partnerships, APAC; multi-city event cadence

likely_objections.objection: How is Orbt different from the many startups already in our salons/programs?
likely_objections.counter: Lead with a concrete shipped-on-Claude artifact + one differentiating metric or hard technical problem solved, not roadmap.

approach_do.point: Open with what you've already built/shipped and a working demo
approach_do.evidence: His programming centers on shipping ('push to prod')

approach_do.point: Connect the ask to his ecosystem/community goals (mutual amplification)
approach_do.evidence: Hosts recurring founder salons and dev events

approach_avoid.point: Don't pitch 'why AI / why Claude' — he already evangelizes it for a living
approach_avoid.evidence: He IS Anthropic's APAC partnerships lead

approach_avoid.point: Don't position as a narrow, single-market one-off if you can show regional relevance

honest_gaps: No 'me'/requester profile provided, so no chemistry section and no verified mutual connections/hooks.
Seed title says 'Software Engineer' but every public source says Head of Startup Partnerships, APAC — confirm his current exact role/scope live.
Location conflict: seed says San Francisco; LinkedIn lists Seoul and his work is APAC-wide — confirm where he's based and which markets he owns.
Almost no personal/background detail beyond Georgetown (s1); his pre-Anthropic career is not in these sources.
Several same-name sources (ophthalmologist Junyeop Lee, K-drama/baseball/poet 'Lee', Lee Kuan Yew) were quarantined as different people and contributed nothing.
No verbatim first-person voice beyond one LinkedIn line; voice/values read is thin.

behavioral_read.method_note: Big Five from observed public behavior; illustrative MBTI only; communication-adaptation, not profiling — traits we couldn't ground are omitted, not guessed.

big_five.signal: Relentless cadence of in-person, public-facing events — moderating panels, hosting salons and hackathons across multiple cities
Builds repeatable programs — recurring salon series and a 'first ever' structured hackathon — not ad hoc meetings
Relationship-forward: publicly credits partners/press and frames work as community-building

behavioral_read.communication_adaptation: Be concrete, fast and builder-to-the-point; he runs on momentum and in-person energy, so a live demo and a clear, mutual win land better than a slow deck-led narrative.
